title: EV Charging JCOM Agreement Approved — MWBE Language and Revenue Model Clarified
summary: Council advances item 46: JCOM agreement for EV charging grant applications at Progress Park. Commissioner Michael Collins explains change from 3 to 2 chargers for faster Level 3 service; site chosen because National Grid recently upgraded the substation. Council secures commitment for MBE/WBE local subcontractor language in future contract. Joe Barry (first assistant corporation counsel) confirms ordinance is contingent on grant receipt with no binding contract with JCOM until grants are awarded. Karina Freeland (sustainability planner) details: no city staff time or match required (median household income qualifies), all costs covered by DEC and National Grid grants, 7.5% networking fee, city controls pricing and receives revenue. First 5 years maintenance with JCOM, city free to contract anyone after. Also: Richard Osber (HR) confirms iSol/Applicant Pro covers all departments citywide (fire and police use civil service list for sworn positions but civilian positions eligible). Council requests executive session on Good Cause Eviction legal specifics.

political_signal.description: Council approves EV charging opportunity after multi-session RFP debate, securing MWBE commitments as condition of support

processing_notes: EV charging debate resolved after 4 study sessions (Feb 4, 9, 18, 23)
JCOM has won 49 of 50 grant applications
Progress Park chosen because substation recently upgraded by National Grid
Ken Tsley (city electrical engineer) confirmed internal staff cannot complete required technical analysis
DEC funds charger equipment, National Grid funds site preparation (Battery Ready Program)
Parks and Fire department detections are false positives from unrelated discussion context
Breanna Stewart 3-on-3 basketball challenge joke from Councilor Caldwell
Segment 7 of 7 from Common Council Study Session, Monday February 23rd 2026
Transcript corrections applied: national grid -> National Grid
